hey cursor, i want to build an app that witll take sound fragments and convert em to text 
Than i want to do this for multiple models and compare wich one is the best. I also want to compare the cost of these models for that fragment 

Can you help me build this app
</user_query>

You want an app that takes audio clips, transcribes them with multiple speech-to-text models, and compares both quality and cost side by side. I’ll first inspect the workspace so I can scaffold this in your project and then implement a working starter with model runners, scoring, and cost estimation.

I found an empty workspace, so next I’ll create a fresh project and scaffold the app from scratch with a backend API + simple UI for upload, multi-model transcription, accuracy comparison, and cost estimation.
I hit a tooling limitation creating the project via MCP, so I’ll proceed directly in the terminal by creating a new local folder and scaffolding the app there.

input.description: Scaffold React TypeScript app with Vite
Check whether project folder was created

content.text: I’m now implementing a runnable MVP in this empty workspace as a Python Streamlit app so you can upload an audio fragment once, run multiple STT models, and get side-by-side transcript quality + estimated cost.

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:\Users\frederic\.cursor\projects\empty-window\README.md
+# STT Model Comparator
+
+A small app to compare speech-to-text models on the same audio fragment.
+
+You can:
+- upload an audio file
+- run several transcription models
+- compare transcripts side-by-side
+- estimate cost per model for the clip
+- score output quality against an optional reference transcript (WER)
+
+## Quick start
+
+1. Create and activate a Python virtual environment.
+2. Install dependencies:
+
+```bash
+pip install -r requirements.txt
+```
+
+3. Configure API keys:
+
+```bash
+set OPENAI_API_KEY=your_openai_key
+set GROQ_API_KEY=your_groq_key
+```
+
+4. Run the app:
+
+```bash
+streamlit run app.py
+```
+
+## Supported providers (MVP)
+
+- OpenAI (`gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, `whisper-1`)
+- Groq (`whisper-large-v3`, `distil-whisper-large-v3-en`)
+
+You can add more models in `model_registry.py`.
+
+## Cost estimation
+
+Costs are estimated with per-minute prices defined in `model_registry.py`.
+Update those values to match your billing contracts/current published pricing.
+
+## Notes
+
+- This MVP computes WER locally if you provide a reference transcript.
+- If no reference is provided, the app still compares runtime, transcript length, and cost.
*** End Patch

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:\Users\frederic\.cursor\projects\empty-window\model_registry.py
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+from typing import Dict, List
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class ModelSpec:
+    id: str
+    provider: str
+    label: str
+    api_model_name: str
+    estimated_price_per_minute_usd: float
+
+
+MODEL_SPECS: List[ModelSpec] = [
+    ModelSpec(
+        id="openai_gpt4o_mini_transcribe",
+        provider="openai",
+        label="OpenAI - gpt-4o-mini-transcribe",
+        api_model_name="gpt-4o-mini-transcribe",
+        estimated_price_per_minute_usd=0.006,
+    ),
+    ModelSpec(
+        id="openai_whisper_1",
+        provider="openai",
+        label="OpenAI - whisper-1",
+        api_model_name="whisper-1",
+        estimated_price_per_minute_usd=0.006,
+    ),
+    ModelSpec(
+        id="groq_whisper_large_v3",
+        provider="groq",
+        label="Groq - whisper-large-v3",
+        api_model_name="whisper-large-v3",
+        estimated_price_per_minute_usd=0.004,
+    ),
+    ModelSpec(
+        id="groq_distil_whisper_large_v3_en",
+        provider="groq",
+        label="Groq - distil-whisper-large-v3-en",
+        api_model_name="distil-whisper-large-v3-en",
+        estimated_price_per_minute_usd=0.002,
+    ),
+]
+
+
+def by_id() -> Dict[str, ModelSpec]:
+    return {m.id: m for m in MODEL_SPECS}
*** End Patch

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:\Users\frederic\.cursor\projects\empty-window\app.py
+import io
+import math
+import os
+import tempfile
+import time
+import wave
+from dataclasses import asdict
+from typing import Dict, List, Optional
+
+import pandas as pd
+import streamlit as st
+from jiwer import wer
+from openai import OpenAI
+
+from model_registry import MODEL_SPECS, ModelSpec, by_id
+
+
+def get_audio_duration_seconds(file_bytes: bytes) -> Optional[float]:
+    # WAV can be measured without external ffmpeg dependency.
+    try:
+        with wave.open(io.BytesIO(file_bytes), "rb") as wav_file:
+            return wav_file.getnframes() / float(wav_file.getframerate())
+    except wave.Error:
+        return None
+
+
+def estimate_cost_usd(duration_seconds: Optional[float], spec: ModelSpec) -> Optional[float]:
+    if duration_seconds is None:
+        return None
+    minutes = duration_seconds / 60.0
+    return minutes * spec.estimated_price_per_minute_usd
+
+
+def transcribe_with_openai(api_key: str, model_name: str, tmp_path: str) -> str:
+    client = OpenAI(api_key=api_key)
+    with open(tmp_path, "rb") as audio_file:
+        response = client.audio.transcriptions.create(
+            model=model_name,
+            file=audio_file,
+            response_format="text",
+        )
+    return str(response)
+
+
+def transcribe_with_groq(api_key: str, model_name: str, tmp_path: str) -> str:
+    # Groq offers an OpenAI-compatible transcription API.
+    client = OpenAI(api_key=api_key, base_url="https://api.groq.com/openai/v1")
+    with open(tmp_path, "rb") as audio_file:
+        response = client.audio.transcriptions.create(
+            model=model_name,
+            file=audio_file,
+            response_format="text",
+        )
+    return str(response)
+
+
+def run_transcription(spec: ModelSpec, tmp_path: str) -> Dict:
+    start = time.perf_counter()
+    transcript = ""
+    error = None
+    try:
+        if spec.provider == "openai":
+            key = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY", "").strip()
+            if not key:
+                raise RuntimeError("Missing OPENAI_API_KEY")
+            transcript = transcribe_with_openai(key, spec.api_model_name, tmp_path)
+        elif spec.provider == "groq":
+            key = os.getenv("GROQ_API_KEY", "").strip()
+            if not key:
+                raise RuntimeError("Missing GROQ_API_KEY")
+            transcript = transcribe_with_groq(key, spec.api_model_name, tmp_path)
+        else:
+            raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported provider: {spec.provider}")
+    except Exception as exc:
+        error = str(exc)
+    elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
+    return {
+        "model_id": spec.id,
+        "provider": spec.provider,
+        "model_label": spec.label,
+        "api_model_name": spec.api_model_name,
+        "runtime_seconds": round(elapsed, 3),
+        "transcript": transcript.strip(),
+        "error": error,
+    }
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    st.set_page_config(page_title="STT Comparator", layout="wide")
+    st.title("Speech-to-Text Model Comparator")
+    st.write(
+        "Upload one audio fragment, run multiple models, compare transcript quality, speed, and estimated cost."
+    )
+
+    all_models = by_id()
+    default_selection = [MODEL_SPECS[0].id, MODEL_SPECS[1].id]
+    selected_ids = st.multiselect(
+        "Choose models to compare",
+        options=[m.id for m in MODEL_SPECS],
+        default=default_selection,
+        format_func=lambda x: all_models[x].label,
+    )
+
+    uploaded = st.file_uploader(
+        "Upload audio",
+        type=["wav", "mp3", "m4a", "flac", "ogg", "webm"],
+        accept_multiple_files=False,
+    )
+
+    reference_text = st.text_area(
+        "Reference transcript (optional, for WER scoring)",
+        placeholder="Paste ground-truth transcript to calculate word error rate.",
+    )
+
+    run = st.button("Run comparison", type="primary", disabled=not uploaded or not selected_ids)
+    if not run:
+        return
+
+    if uploaded is None:
+        st.warning("Please upload an audio file.")
+        return
+
+    file_bytes = uploaded.read()
+    duration_seconds = get_audio_duration_seconds(file_bytes)
+
+    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False, suffix=f"_{uploaded.name}") as tmp:
+        tmp.write(file_bytes)
+        temp_audio_path = tmp.name
+
+    results: List[Dict] = []
+    progress = st.progress(0, text="Running models...")
+    for i, model_id in enumerate(selected_ids, start=1):
+        spec = all_models[model_id]
+        result = run_transcription(spec, temp_audio_path)
+        result["estimated_cost_usd"] = estimate_cost_usd(duration_seconds, spec)
+        if reference_text.strip() and not result["error"] and result["transcript"]:
+            result["wer"] = wer(reference_text, result["transcript"])
+        else:
+            result["wer"] = None
+        results.append(result)
+        progress.progress(i / len(selected_ids), text=f"Completed {i}/{len(selected_ids)} models")
+
+    os.unlink(temp_audio_path)
+
+    rows = []
+    for r in results:
+        rows.append(
+            {
+                "Model": r["model_label"],
+                "Runtime (s)": r["runtime_seconds"],
+                "Estimated Cost (USD)": None
+                if r["estimated_cost_usd"] is None
+                else round(r["estimated_cost_usd"], 6),
+                "WER (lower is better)": None if r["wer"] is None else round(r["wer"], 4),
+                "Status": "OK" if not r["error"] else f"ERROR: {r['error']}",
+                "Transcript chars": len(r["transcript"]) if r["transcript"] else 0,
+            }
+        )
+
+    st.subheader("Comparison table")
+    st.dataframe(pd.DataFrame(rows), use_container_width=True)
+
+    if duration_seconds is None:
+        st.info(
+            "Could not detect audio duration from this format. Cost is shown as blank. "
+            "WAV duration is detected natively in this MVP."
+        )
+    else:
+        st.caption(f"Detected clip duration: {round(duration_seconds, 2)} seconds")
+
+    st.subheader("Transcripts")
+    for r in results:
+        with st.expander(r["model_label"], expanded=False):
+            if r["error"]:
+                st.error(r["error"])
+            else:
+                st.write(r["transcript"] or "(empty)")
+
+    valid = [r for r in results if not r["error"]]
+    if valid:
+        st.subheader("Best model hint")
+        scored = []
+        for r in valid:
+            # If WER exists, prioritize quality first; otherwise speed + cost.
+            quality_score = r["wer"] if r["wer"] is not None else 1.0
+            cost_score = r["estimated_cost_usd"] if r["estimated_cost_usd"] is not None else 999.0
+            speed_score = r["runtime_seconds"]
+            combined = (quality_score * 0.6) + (cost_score * 0.3) + (speed_score * 0.1)
+            scored.append((combined, r))
+        scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
+        winner = scored[0][1]
+        st.success(
+            f"Suggested winner: {winner['model_label']} "
+            f"(runtime={winner['runtime_seconds']}s, "
+            f"cost={winner['estimated_cost_usd']}, wer={winner['wer']})"
+        )
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()
*** End Patch

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: C:\Users\frederic\.cursor\projects\empty-window\app.py

content.text: I’ve finished the core implementation and I’m quickly sanity-checking it for syntax/runtime issues before sharing run instructions and next improvements.

Built you a working MVP app in the workspace that does exactly this comparison workflow.

### What I implemented

- Created a Streamlit app at `app.py` that:
  - uploads one audio fragment
  - runs transcription against multiple STT models
  - compares runtime, transcript output, and estimated cost
  - computes WER (word error rate) when you provide a reference transcript
  - suggests a “best model” based on quality + cost + speed
- Added a model registry at `model_registry.py` with editable providers/models/prices.
- Added dependencies in `requirements.txt`.
- Added setup and usage docs in `README.md`.
- Ran a syntax check (`python -m py_compile`) successfully.

### Files added

- `README.md`
- `requirements.txt`
- `model_registry.py`
- `app.py`

### Run it

From the project root:

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
set OPENAI_API_KEY=your_openai_key
set GROQ_API_KEY=your_groq_key
streamlit run app.py
```

### Notes

- Cost is estimated using per-minute pricing in `model_registry.py` (easy to edit).
- Duration detection is native for WAV in this MVP; other formats still transcribe but may show blank cost unless you convert to WAV first.
- You can extend this to more providers quickly by adding more provider functions + entries in `MODEL_SPECS`.

If you want, next I can add:
- automatic duration detection for all formats (via `ffmpeg`/`pydub`),
- export results to CSV/JSON,
- better scoring dashboard (charts + rank by your custom weights),
- and batch testing over a folder of clips (for real benchmark runs).

<timestamp>Monday, May 4, 2026, 2:33 PM (UTC+2)</timestamp>
<user_query>
yes it worked, thanks
Now how can i run this say in vercel?
</user_query>

Nice — great to hear it worked.

Short version: **your current Streamlit app won’t run well on Vercel directly**.  
Vercel is best for serverless/Next.js, not always-on Python web servers like Streamlit.

## Best options

- **Fastest deploy (keep current code):** use Streamlit Community Cloud / Render / Railway.
- **Use Vercel anyway:** split app into:
  - **Frontend** (Next.js) on Vercel
  - **Backend API** (Python/FastAPI or Node) on Vercel functions *or* another host

## If you want Vercel specifically

### Option A (recommended for Vercel): Rebuild UI in Next.js
1. Create Next.js app.
2. Add upload form.
3. Create API route (`/api/transcribe`) that:
   - accepts audio
   - calls OpenAI/Groq transcription APIs
   - returns transcripts + cost estimates
4. Deploy to Vercel.
5. Add env vars in Vercel project settings:
   - `OPENAI_API_KEY`
   - `GROQ_API_KEY`

### Option B: Keep Python logic, serve as API functions
- Move transcription logic from `app.py` into a Python function endpoint (FastAPI/Flask style).
- Keep frontend separate (can still be Vercel).
- Watch out for function timeout/upload limits.

---

If you want, I can do this next step for you: **convert your current Streamlit MVP into a Vercel-ready Next.js + API version** with the same compare table and cost scoring.

<timestamp>Monday, May 4, 2026, 2:33 PM (UTC+2)</timestamp>
<user_query>
i have railway also
</user_query>

Perfect — Railway is a great fit for your current Streamlit app (no rewrite needed).

## Deploy your current app to Railway

1. In your repo, make sure these files exist:
- `app.py`
- `requirements.txt`

2. Add a `Procfile` at project root:

```txt
web: streamlit run app.py --server.port $PORT --server.address 0.0.0.0
```

3. (Optional but recommended) add `.streamlit/config.toml`:

```toml
[server]
headless = true
enableCORS = false
enableXsrfProtection = false
```

4. Push these changes to GitHub.

5. In Railway:
- New Project -> Deploy from GitHub repo
- Select your `speach-to-text` repo
- Railway will detect Python and install from `requirements.txt`

6. Add environment variables in Railway project settings:
- `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- `GROQ_API_KEY`

7. Deploy and open the generated Railway URL.

## Quick notes

- Your app handles transcription on-demand, so Railway free/basic tiers usually work fine to start.
- If you upload large audio files, you may need higher memory/time limits later.
- Cost estimation values are in `model_registry.py`; update those anytime pricing changes.

If you want, I can give you the exact `git add/commit/push` commands for the `Procfile` + config in one copy-paste block.

<timestamp>Monday, May 4, 2026, 2:38 PM (UTC+2)</timestamp>
<user_query>
i dont see the repo in my railway
</user_query>

That’s usually a GitHub-Railway connection/permission issue, not your code.

Try this quick checklist:

- In Railway: `New Project` -> `Deploy from GitHub repo`
- If your repo is missing, click `Configure GitHub App` (or reconnect GitHub account)
- In GitHub App permissions, make sure Railway has access to:
  - either **All repositories**
  - or specifically `fredericVDK/speach-to-text`
- Confirm the repo is on GitHub (open it in browser and refresh)
- In Railway, refresh the repo picker after granting access

If it still doesn’t appear:

- Disconnect GitHub from Railway and reconnect
- During reconnect, choose the correct GitHub account (`fredericVDK`)
- Explicitly grant access to that repo
- Retry `Deploy from GitHub repo`

Fallback (works immediately):
- In Railway, choose template/empty service and connect via **public GitHub URL** if prompted, or use Railway CLI deploy.

If you want, I can walk you click-by-click through the exact GitHub App screen settings next.

<timestamp>Monday, May 4, 2026, 2:40 PM (UTC+2)</timestamp>
<user_query>
After push, in Railway just connect repo + add env vars:

OPENAI_API_KEY
GROQ_API_KEY

Where can i find these api keys
</user_query>

- `OPENAI_API_KEY`: create it in OpenAI dashboard at [platform.openai.com/api-keys](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys)
- `GROQ_API_KEY`: create it in Groq console at [console.groq.com/keys](https://console.groq.com/keys)

Then in Railway:
- Go to your service -> `Variables`
- Add:
  - `OPENAI_API_KEY` = your OpenAI key
  - `GROQ_API_KEY` = your Groq key
- Redeploy (or Railway auto-redeploys)

Quick note: both providers usually require billing/project setup before keys work.  
If you want, I can also show how to make the app work when only one key is set (so it skips missing providers instead of erroring).

<timestamp>Monday, May 4, 2026, 2:57 PM (UTC+2)</timestamp>
<user_query>
are there free speach to text api's because these are paid plans
</user_query>

Yes — there are some free options, with trade-offs.

### Free / low-cost options

- **Open-source local models (best truly free):**
  - `faster-whisper` (Whisper models) running on your own machine/server
  - No per-minute API cost, but you pay compute (or use your laptop)
- **Hugging Face Inference (limited free tier on some accounts/models):**
  - Can work, but quotas/rate limits are small
- **Browser Web Speech API (Chrome-based):**
  - Free to use in browser, but less reliable/consistent for benchmarking
- **Some providers offer trial credits** (not fully free long-term):
  - Deepgram, AssemblyAI, etc. often have starter credits

### Best for your app right now

If you want to compare models **without API bills**, add a **local Whisper backend**:
- Keep your current app
- Add local transcription via `faster-whisper`
- Compare local model variants (`tiny`, `base`, `small`, `medium`, `large-v3`)
- Cost can be shown as `$0 API` (or estimated compute cost)

If you want, I can modify your app now to support:
- local `faster-whisper` models (free),
- plus optional paid APIs when keys are available,
- so you can benchmark both in one UI.

 import pandas as pd
 import streamlit as st
+from faster_whisper import WhisperModel

 from openai import OpenAI
@@
 def transcribe_with_groq(api_key: str, model_name: str, tmp_path: str) -> str:
@@
     return str(response)
 
 
+@st.cache_resource(show_spinner=False)
+def load_local_whisper_model(model_size: str) -> WhisperModel:
+    return WhisperModel(model_size, device="cpu", compute_type="int8")
+
+
+def transcribe_with_local_faster_whisper(model_name: str, tmp_path: str) -> str:
+    model = load_local_whisper_model(model_name)
+    segments, _info = model.transcribe(tmp_path)
+    return " ".join(s.text.strip() for s in segments).strip()
+
+
 def run_transcription(spec: ModelSpec, tmp_path: str) -> Dict:
@@
         elif spec.provider == "groq":
             key = os.getenv("GROQ_API_KEY", "").strip()
             if not key:
                 raise RuntimeError("Missing GROQ_API_KEY")
             transcript = transcribe_with_groq(key, spec.api_model_name, tmp_path)
+        elif spec.provider == "local":
+            transcript = transcribe_with_local_faster_whisper(spec.api_model_name, tmp_path)

             raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported provider: {spec.provider}")

content.text:
Done — I added free local STT models so you can test without paid API keys.

### What changed

- `requirements.txt`
  - added `faster-whisper`
- `model_registry.py`
  - added local free models:
    - `tiny`
    - `base`
    - `small`
  - cost set to `0.0` for local models
- `app.py`
  - added local transcription provider using `faster-whisper`
  - added cached model loading for speed
  - made local models the default selected models
- `README.md`
  - updated docs to show API keys are optional
  - documented local free provider support

### Important behavior now

- No `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GROQ_API_KEY` needed to run local models.
- You can still enable paid models later by adding keys.
- Local models may be slower (depends on machine).

### Next steps for you

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
streamlit run app.py
```

Then choose local models in the UI and test with an audio clip.

If you want, I can also add a UI filter toggle like “Free only / Paid only / All” to make testing easier.

Given the terminal in this session is not returning visible git output, the safest verification on your side is:

```bash
git log --oneline -n 3
git status
```

and check GitHub for the latest commit.

Then in Railway:
- trigger a redeploy (or it auto-deploys on push),
- no API keys needed for local model testing (you can leave `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GROQ_API_KEY` empty for now),
- open app and select the `Local (free)` models.

If you want, I can now also add a small “provider filter” in the UI (`Local only / Paid only / All`) before you test.
